name: Stickynote Workflow
description: Automated workflow: Stickynote Workflow. This workflow integrates 9 different services: stickyNote, code, gmailTrigger, agent, outputParserStructured. It contains 14 nodes and follows best practices for error handling and security.

nodes.name: Sticky Note3
nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: AI Agent

options.systemMessage:
=You are an assistant that processes emails related to inbound orders from Hermas.

Each email has the subject line containing a purchase order reference (e.g., "PO45231").
In the email body, you will find:

An expected delivery date, typically in formats like 27/03/2025 or 2025-03-27.

One or more order lines, where each line contains:

An SKU (e.g., HERM-SHOE-001)

A quantity (e.g., 120)

Your goal is to extract the following fields:

purchase_order: The PO number from the subject line (e.g., PO45231)

expected_delivery_date: In ISO format (e.g., 2025-03-27)

lines: A list of objects with sku and quantity for each order line

Return your output strictly as a valid JSON object using the format below.

nodes.notes: This agent node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: OpenAI Chat Model
nodes.notes: This lmChatOpenAi node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Structured Output Parser
nodes.notes: This outputParserStructured node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note1

parameters.content:
### 1. Workflow Trigger with Gmail Trigger
The workflow is triggered by a new email received in your Gmail mailbox. 
If the subject includes the string "Inbound Order" we proceed, if not we do nothing.

#### How to setup?
- **Gmail Trigger Node:** set up your Gmail API credentials
[Learn more about the Gmail Trigger Node]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note2

parameters.content:
### 2. AI Agent equipped with the query tool
The email body and subject are sent to the AI agent for parsing. The results include the **PO Number**, **expected delivery date** and all the order lines with **SKU ID** and **order quantity**. Outputs are formatted by the code node to fit in a Google Sheet.

#### How to setup?
- **AI Agent with the Chat Model**:
   1. Add a **chat model** with the required credentials *(Example: Open AI 4o-mini)*
   2. Adapt the system prompt to the format of your emails
  [Learn more about the AI Agent Node]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note4

parameters.content:
### 3. Store the orderlines in a Google Sheet
The table generated by the **code node** includes all the order lines with the **PO Number** and the **expected delivery date**. This **Google Sheet Node** loads the content in a Google Sheet.

#### How to setup?
- **Add Results in Google Sheets**:
   1. Add your Google Sheet API credentials to access the Google Sheet file
   2. Select the file using the list, an URL or an ID
   3. Select the sheet in which the vocabulary list is stored
   4. Create the columns: **PO_NUMBER, EXPECTED_DELIVERY DATE, SKU_ID, QUANTITY**
  [Learn more about the Google Sheet Node]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

parameters.content:
### Test the workflow with this email!

#### How?
1. Send this email to the Gmail box you set up in your credentials.
2. Click on Test workflow

### Email
**Email Subject:** Inbound Order PO45231 – Expected Delivery on 2025-03-27

**Email Body:** 
Dear LogiGreen Team,

Please find below the details of the upcoming inbound order.

Purchase Order: PO45231
Expected Delivery Date: 27/03/2025

Order Lines:

SKU: HERM-SHOE-001 — Qty: 120

SKU: HERM-BAG-032 — Qty: 45

SKU: HERM-WATCH-105 — Qty: 30

SKU: HERM-SCARF-018 — Qty: 80

Let us know if you need any additional details.

Best regards,
Sophie Lambert
Admin Assistant – Hermas Logistics
📞 +33 1 23 45 67 89 78 84
✉️ sophie.lambert@hermas.com

### Email

Dear LogiGreen Team,

### 1. Workflow Trigger with Gmail Trigger
The workflow is triggered by a new email received in your Gmail mailbox. 
If the subject includes the string "Inbound Order" we proceed, if not we do nothing.

#### How to setup?
- **Gmail Trigger Node:** set up your Gmail API credentials
[Learn more about the Gmail Trigger Node]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

### 2. AI Agent equipped with the query tool
The email body and subject are sent to the AI agent for parsing. The results include the **PO Number**, **expected delivery date** and all the order lines with **SKU ID** and **order quantity**. Outputs are formatted by the code node to fit in a Google Sheet.

#### How to setup?
- **AI Agent with the Chat Model**:
   1. Add a **chat model** with the required credentials *(Example: Open AI 4o-mini)*
   2. Adapt the system prompt to the format of your emails
  [Learn more about the AI Agent Node]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

### 3. Store the orderlines in a Google Sheet
The table generated by the **code node** includes all the order lines with the **PO Number** and the **expected delivery date**. This **Google Sheet Node** loads the content in a Google Sheet.

#### How to setup?
- **Add Results in Google Sheets**:
   1. Add your Google Sheet API credentials to access the Google Sheet file
   2. Select the file using the list, an URL or an ID
   3. Select the sheet in which the vocabulary list is stored
   4. Create the columns: **PO_NUMBER, EXPECTED_DELIVERY DATE, SKU_ID, QUANTITY**
  [Learn more about the Google Sheet Node]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: No custom credentials required for utility-only operations.
configurationSteps: Verify the settings of the trigger node "Email Received".
Open the "Sticky Note3" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "AI Agent" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "OpenAI Chat Model" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Structured Output Parser" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note1" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note2" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note4" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Store Purchase Order Lines" Google Sheets node.
Ensure you select the spreadsheet file and specific tab/sheet from the dropdown menus.
Map the input JSON keys to your target sheet columns.
Open the "Is PO?" node (n8n-nodes-base.if) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Error Handler" node (n8n-nodes-base.stopAndError) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
No global variables node (Set node) is present. If you need dynamic constants, add a Set node after the trigger.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Incoming triggers will qualified/formatted, new rows will be written to Google Sheets, and email replies or alerts will be sent automatically.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
